summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting covering zoning text amendments, a proposed new pole barn for the service department, fire department promotions, and the chief's acceptance to the National Fire Academy executive officer program. The board approved temporary dwelling zoning text amendments and initiated text amendments on accessory structures including shipping container regulations. Two part-time firefighter/paramedics were promoted to full-time, and a fund transfer for Station 18 architectural fees was tabled pending confirmation of restricted fund transfer rules.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda – passed unanimously
Motion to approve payment of bills (March 27 – April 9, 2024) – passed unanimously
Zoning case ZC-2024-003 (temporary dwelling regulations text amendments) – approved unanimously
Resolution R-006-2024 to initiate zoning text amendments on accessory structures – approved unanimously
Motion to promote part-time firefighter/paramedic Elizabeth Hyland to full-time effective April 21, 2024 – passed unanimously
Motion to promote part-time firefighter/paramedic Harley Hargis to full-time with lateral transfer of sick leave at step three effective April 21, 2024 – passed unanimously
Motion to approve Chief Edward Myers to attend the Executive Fire Officer Program at the National Fire Academy at no cost – passed unanimously
Item I (transfer of $8,785.18 from Fund 2193 to Fund 4901 for Station 18) – tabled pending verification of restricted fund transfer rules
Item J (related to Station 18 architectural fees) – tabled
Motion to adjourn at 6:48 PM – passed unanimously
action_items: Chad Meadows to obtain gas line estimate for the proposed pole barn and report back at the next meeting
Chad Meadows to verify with Permit Central that the pole barn construction is compliant with all requirements
Chad Meadows to check with wastewater department on wash bay regulations
Lisa/fiscal officer to verify whether transferring from restricted Fund 2193 to Fund 4901 is permissible before next meeting
Bob Rose (Administrator) to be present at next meeting to discuss pole barn funding and fund transfer items
Liz (zoning) to submit accessory structure text amendments to Zoning Commission for review
Liz to revise zoning resolution for temporary dwelling amendments, effective 30 days from April 9
key_amounts: $40,000 for 31x81 pole barn ordered and installed
$14,000 for electrical installation to the pole barn
$85,000 total proposed cost for pole barn project ($80,000 building + $5,000 contingency)
$61,615 remaining in ARPA fund if all undecided projects are approved
$1.8 million originally received in ARPA federal funds
$8,785.18 proposed transfer from Fund 2193 to Fund 4901 for Station 18
$2.5 million capital budget request in House bill for fire station
$2.5 million capital budget request in Senate bill for fire station
67% of 9-1-1 funds to Clermont County Commissioners, 27% to Union Township, 6% to Northeast division
notable_topics: Zoning text amendments for temporary dwelling regulations (ZC-2024-003) approved
Zoning text amendments for accessory structures and shipping container regulations initiated
Proposed new 5-bay pole barn for service department discussed with ARPA funding
Promotion of two part-time firefighter/paramedics (Hyland and Hargis) to full-time
Chief Myers accepted into National Fire Academy Executive Fire Officer Program
Capital budget bills for new fire station update – passed House, pending Senate
New generation 9-1-1 system contract signed for Clermont County
Station 18 building fund transfer tabled for verification
ARPA fund explanation for public transparency
attendees: Bob Rose (Township Administrator, absent/under the weather)
Tyler Harmon (from Representative Smith's office, mentioned)
notes: Administrator Bob Rose was absent due to illness. Chris in the service department celebrated his one-year anniversary and completed CDL training. The meeting was notably short, adjourning at 6:48 PM. Chief Myers was accepted into the National Fire Academy Executive Fire Officer Program on his third application attempt. The board discussed that the old pole barn was removed in 2019 and the new one would be a replacement in the same location. Sycamore Township and City of Montgomery have expressed interest in seeing the completed pole barn project.
title: BOT 04.09.2024
